prompt: A OmniStock Warehouse & Catalog management system that includes inventory location control and on-site product content governance for organizations running a closed, offline network such as a district warehouse supporting multiple schools and departments. Users include Warehouse Clerks who receive, move, and pick inventory; Catalog Editors who manage item details and respond to questions; Moderators who review user-generated content; Managers who monitor throughput and compliance; and Administrators who control access. The Angular web interface is optimized for speed: a global search workspace supports combined filters across item, lot, warehouse, document status, and time range, returning paginated results with sortable columns and saved views per user. Clerks can scan barcodes using a USB scanner (keyboard-wedge) or device camera input to jump directly to an item/lot record and execute allowed actions with immediate inline validation feedback. Bulk processing supports guided import and export of CSV/XLSX files using downloadable templates; the UI shows pre-check results (missing required fields, invalid units, duplicate barcodes), allows users to fix and re-upload, and displays a per-row outcome report after processing. Warehouse setup screens manage a warehouse–zone–bin hierarchy with bin attributes such as temperature band, max load in pounds, and dimensional limits in inches, and users can enable/disable bins with a visible change timeline. Product content pages support star ratings, text reviews, image attachments from local files, follow-up reviews, Q&A threads, favorites, and browsing history, plus an abuse report flow that informs the reporter of case status through an in-app inbox rather than email. On the system side, the backend uses Fastify to expose a RESTful API consumed by the Angular frontend over the local network, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for inventory, location, document workflows, content, and audit trails. Authentication is local username/password only, enforcing 12+ character passwords, complexity rules, and password history of the last 5; failed logins lock the account for 15 minutes after 7 attempts, and CAPTCHA is generated and validated locally. Authorization combines RBAC with attribute rules (for example, a Clerk can only view rows for assigned warehouses and cannot export content images), and every permission change, login, export, moderation action, and bin enable/disable is written to an immutable audit log. Integrations are supported only with other on-prem systems via signed REST endpoints and optional webhooks delivered to internal URLs; requests require HMAC signature verification, per-client rate limits (e.g., 120 requests/minute), and isolated API scopes to prevent cross-department data leakage. Imports run in transactions with rollback on validation failure, while successful jobs write a detailed results table for traceability; scheduled batch jobs generate operational metrics (put-away time, pick accuracy, review resolution SLA) nightly at 2:00 AM and archive completed documents older than 365 days. Sensitive fields are masked by role in query responses, files are stored on local disk with checksum verification, and PostgreSQL encryption functions are used for high-risk fields; passwords are hashed with Argon2id and sessions are rotated and revocable to keep the entire deployment secure and fully functional without any internet dependency.
